vibe: Late-night Tokyo to London — dub house grooves, city pop warmth, UK jazz energy, and math-rock precision

tracks.name: Do it Again
tracks.reason: You've been deep in Soichi Terada lately — Yokota is his Tokyo house counterpart. This one's a hypnotic, stripped-back groove from the Far East scene that should slot right in after those Deepchord sessions.

tracks.name: Abusey Junction
tracks.reason: With Nubya Garcia and Yussef Dayes in heavy rotation, Kokoroko is the afro-jazz side of that same London scene. Warm horns, gentle pace — proper Tuesday morning energy.

tracks.name: POOL
tracks.reason: You've been playing toe — tricot is their spiritual successor. All-female Japanese math-rock with insane time signatures that somehow still make you want to nod your head.

tracks.name: Snap My Finger (feat. PinkPantheress)
tracks.reason: Bridges the gap between your &ME/Emanuel Satie electronic picks and the pop sensibility of your hip-hop rotation. KAYTRANADA's production is surgical — PinkPantheress floats on it.

tracks.name: Glass no Sougen
tracks.reason: You had Tomoko Aran on recently — Momoko Kikuchi is that same 80s city pop world. Lush synths, dreamy vocals, pure late-night Tokyo nostalgia while you're actually in Tokyo.

tracks.name: Archangel
tracks.reason: You're deep in the Floating Points / Basic Channel / Deepchord zone — Burial is the UK garage side of that same rainy-window-at-2am energy. If you haven't revisited this in a while, it still hits different.

tracks.name: City Lights (feat. Pase Rock & Substantial)
tracks.reason: Lo-fi pioneer, Tokyo legend. With you being in Japan right now and the hip-hop in your top tracks, Nujabes threads the needle perfectly — jazzy, mellow, and distinctly Tokyo.

tracks.name: What Am I to Do?
tracks.reason: Same London jazz circuit as Nubya Garcia and Yussef Dayes, but Ezra Collective bring more Afrobeat swagger. This one's got a groove that doesn't let go.

tracks.name: MONSTRUO
tracks.reason: Rauw Alejandro was in your recent plays — Tainy is the producer behind half the best reggaeton tracks out there. This one's darker and more experimental than typical reggaeton, production-forward.

tracks.name: Broken
tracks.reason: Instrumental soul/funk in the Surprise Chef lane — one-man project making lush, cinematic grooves. Perfect palette cleanser between the heavier electronic stuff and the jazz.
